answer:
Well, in my free time, I absolutely love playing the guitar. It's something I picked up a few years ago, and honestly, it's been such a rewarding experience. Initially, I struggled a bit with getting my fingers to move the way I wanted them to, but after practicing regularly, I've noticed a significant improvement. You know, it's like finding a new part of myself that I didn't know existed. 

I often play with a couple of my friends, who share this hobby with me. We usually get together on weekends to jam and try out new songs. It's not just about music for us; it's really brought us closer and created some amazing memories. I mean, we can sit for hours just playing and laughing, completely losing track of time. 

Also, I've found that collaborating with others has helped me improve even faster. They introduce me to different styles and techniques that I wouldn't have explored on my own. For example, one of my friends is really into blues, and he taught me some cool riffs that have stretched my musical range. It's fun to see how our different tastes influence each other and push us to try new things.

So, overall, playing the guitar isn't just a hobby for me; it's been a way to connect with my friends and express myself creatively. I'm really excited to continue growing in this area as I head off to university and hopefully join a band or something similar.
title: What do you like doing in your free time? How have you improved at it? Who shares this hobby with you and how has it brought you closer?
